headline: Legal Drama 'War' Expands Star Roster for High-Stakes Season 2
lede: Sky and HBO's intense legal thriller is doubling down on talent with a powerhouse ensemble for its sophomore season, adding depth to an already compelling narrative.
body:
Sky's provocative legal drama 'War' continues its strategic casting assault, securing three heavyweight performers for its upcoming second season. Marisa Tomei, Kelly Macdonald, and David Harewood join the previously announced cast of Dominic West and Sienna Miller, signaling serious network confidence in the series.

The new additions suggest the show will further explore complex legal and personal dynamics that defined its freshman run. Tomei, an Oscar winner with razor-sharp dramatic chops, represents a particularly intriguing recruitment. Her ability to portray nuanced, ambitious professionals makes her an ideal fit for the series' high-stakes legal landscape.

Macdonald and Harewood bring their own substantial dramatic credentials to the project. Macdonald, known for her understated yet powerful performances, and Harewood, with his commanding screen presence, promise to inject additional narrative complexity into the show's already intricate storytelling.

While specific character details remain under wraps, industry insiders suggest these casting moves indicate an ambitious expansion of the series' narrative scope. The ensemble continues to position 'War' as a serious contender in the increasingly competitive prestige drama market.

Production is expected to commence later this year, with the new season likely targeting a mid-2027 premiere window. Sky and HBO are maintaining their strategic approach of assembling top-tier talent to distinguish the series in an increasingly crowded streaming landscape.
key_facts: Marisa Tomei, Kelly Macdonald, David Harewood join cast
Second season confirmed before first season launch
Dominic West and Sienna Miller returning
